result:
If I could change the world in one way, I'd make every human mind start with an unbreakable default: a genuine, automatic desire to understand reality as it actually is, rather than as they wish it to be.

Everything else—cooperation, progress, reduced suffering, better decisions—would follow from that single shift. Most of our biggest problems aren't lack of resources or intelligence. They're the endless layers of self-deception, tribal signaling, and comforting lies we build on top of the truth. Remove the incentive to prefer the story over the evidence, and the rest starts correcting itself.

message.content:

Everything else—cooperation, progress, reduced suffering, better decisions—would follow from that single shift. Most of our biggest problems aren't lack of resources or intelligence. They're the endless layers of self-deception, tribal signaling, and comforting lies we build on top of the truth. Remove the incentive to prefer the story over the evidence, and the rest starts correcting itself.
message.reasoning_content: If I could change the world in one way, I'd make truth and understanding the top priorities for everyone, over comfort or bias. That would speed up progress and cut down on conflicts by letting people agree on facts.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
